task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly calculates the trimmed mean by dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and maximum values.
trace: First, I sorted the list in ascending order to make it easier to identify minimum and maximum occurrences. Then, I calculated the number of occurrences for both the minimum and maximum values. If either value has more than one occurrence, I remove those values from the list while ensuring not to remove the first or last element. Finally, I calculate and return the mean of the remaining elements as a float. This function handles an edge case where either the minimum or maximum value might appear more than once in the list.
